title: The Impact of Employee Performance and Reward Management on Job Satisfaction in Pakistan
author: Saleem, Aamer
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The Investigating the link between employee performance and reward management, specifically  focusing on work satisfaction in Pakistan, is a captivating subject. This field of research involves  several facets, including the influence of diverse incentive systems on employee morale, job  satisfaction, and overall performance within the organizational framework of Pakistan. The  management of rewards and the performance of employees are crucial elements in every business  since they have a substantial impact on the firm's overall success. Employee performance is  significantly impacted by job satisfaction, which may be affected by several factors, including  incentive management. This paper seeks to thoroughly examine work satisfaction in Pakistan, with a  specific emphasis on the influence of reward management on employee performance. The research  methodology for this study adopted both qualitative and quantitative methods. The qualitative  approach is a systematic literature review while the quantitative method is experimental research,  which involves manipulating variables related to reward management and assessing their impact on  employee performance. This research examines the correlation between reward management and  employee performance, specifically emphasizing satisfaction with jobs in Pakistan. The study results  may be a beneficial point of reference for firms operating in Pakistan to create and execute efficient  rewards management systems that can improve employee performance and job satisfaction
